batch

Class MediaBatchProcessor

  • java.lang.Object
    • batch.MediaBatchProcessor

  • public final class MediaBatchProcessor
extends java.lang.Object
    Automates the batch processing of image files by copying, renaming, and chronologically sorting them, typically based on their DateTimeOriginal EXIF metadata.

    This processor implements a "surgical" strategy: it never modifies source files. Instead, it creates a renamed copy in the target directory and applies binary patches to the metadata segments of the copy to ensure chronological integrity across JPEG, TIFF, PNG, WebP, and HEIF formats.

    A built-in 10-second offset is applied to user-defined dates to prevent metadata collisions and ensure stable sorting in downstream applications (like Windows Photos or Apple Photos).

    Since:
    5 May 2026
    Version:
    1.1
    Author:
    Trevor Maggs

    • Constructor Detail

      • MediaBatchProcessor

        public MediaBatchProcessor(BatchConfiguration config,
                           MetadataScanner scanner)
        Constructs a new Processor using the specified configuration and media scanner.
        Parameters:
        config - the configuration settings used to initialise the executor
        scanner - the scanner containing the discovered media records to process

    • Method Detail

      • addProgressListener

        public void addProgressListener(progressbar.ProgressListener listener)
        Registers a listener used to respond to progress updates during the batch execution.
        Parameters:
        listener - the progress listener to add

      • execute

        public final void execute()
                   throws BatchErrorException
        Begins the batch processing workflow by preparing the target directory, setting up logging, and processing the specified source files or directory.

        Note, this method is final to ensure no subclass accidentally overrides the defined critical logic.

        Throws:
        BatchErrorException - if an I/O error has occurred during directory preparation or file processing

      • notifyListeners

        private void notifyListeners(int current,
                             int total)
        Iterates through registered listeners to broadcast the current progress.
        Parameters:
        current - the number of files processed
        total - the total number of files in the batch

      • processRecord

        private void processRecord(MediaRecord record,
                           int index,
                           int total)
                    throws BatchErrorException
        Handles the end-to-end processing of a single media record.

        The process follows a strict "copy-then-patch" sequence:

        1. Calculate the effective timestamp (Natural vs. User-defined).
        2. Generate a new filename based on configuration.
        3. Copy the source file to the target location.
        4. Apply binary metadata patches to the copy if forced.
        5. Update file-system attributes (Last Modified Time).
        Parameters:
        record - the media file record to process
        index - the current position in the batch
        total - the total number of files in the batch
        Throws:
        BatchErrorException - if file I/O or metadata patching fails

      • calculateEffectiveTime

        private java.nio.file.attribute.FileTime calculateEffectiveTime(MediaRecord record,
                                                                int index)
        Determines the final timestamp for the record, applying increments if the date is user-defined.
        Parameters:
        record - the media record being processed
        index - the current index used to calculate the 10-second offset
        Returns:
        the calculated FileTime for metadata and file-system updates

      • generateTargetName

        private java.lang.String generateTargetName(MediaRecord record,
                                            int index,
                                            java.nio.file.attribute.FileTime time)
        Constructs the target filename using prefix, date/time embedding, and index padding.
        Parameters:
        record - the media record
        index - the batch index for numerical padding, such as 001, 002, etc
        time - the timestamp to embed if enabled
        Returns:
        a formatted string representing the new filename

      • prepareTargetDirectory

        private void prepareTargetDirectory()
                             throws BatchErrorException
        Prepares the target directory by ensuring it exists and is empty.

        Safety Check: Throws an exception if the target is identical to the source to prevent accidental data deletion during the destructive clean phase.

        Throws:
        BatchErrorException - if source/target are identical or directory creation fails

      • startLogging

        private void startLogging()
                   throws BatchErrorException
        Begins the logging system and writes configuration details to a log file. This method is for internal setup and is not intended for external use.
        Throws:
        BatchErrorException - if the logging service cannot be established
